primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intragroup foreign currency risk for hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: This paragraph explicitly prohibits designating intragroup items as hedged items in consolidated financial statements, requiring hedged items to be with parties external to the reporting entity

This paragraph provides the exception for intragroup monetary items in consolidated statements, but only for foreign currency risk arising from differences in functional currencies between group entities

supporting_authority.reason: This Interpretation confirms that hedge accounting for net investments applies only to foreign exchange differences between functional currencies of the foreign operation and parent entity's functional currency

peripheral_authority.reason: IAS 24 addresses related party transaction disclosures but does not govern hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup items
IFRS 12 addresses disclosures about subsidiaries and structured entities but does not affect hedge accounting treatment for intragroup items

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting prohibition for intragroup items in consolidated financial statements
Limited exception for foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items

approaches.label: Hedge accounting prohibited for intragroup foreign exchange risk in consolidated statements
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 explicitly requires hedged items to involve parties external to the reporting entity, which precludes hedge accounting for intragroup dividend receivables in consolidated financial statements

approaches.label: Exception for foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items affecting consolidated profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 provides a narrow exception permitting foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items as hedged items in consolidated statements when the risk is not fully eliminated on consolidation and affects consolidated profit or loss
